pages.full_text:
For May half-term need woolly hats, barbeque, meat, charcoal, a loaf a day, tinned milk, loo paper
at 2.30. it was full. So we drove on looking for a place to make out our own lunch in the shade, but the gorge was so steep we didn't find one. Finally we found a restaurant + had lunch at 3.30.
		We've stopped in our last campsite in Spain. It is very clean + has a municipal pool nearby. __Najera__		**** We swam + Thomas + Ster rowed, so we are not looking around the town. The best campsite we've been in this holiday - We are in a circular courtyard.
		After supper we went out for a walk + joined the promenaders. Festive crowds in the town, John + Charles + I joined in some dancing.
	Mon 13th
	We got up at 8 + left at 9.45. Through terraced Rioja country. Unfortunately it rained + wet the tent. We reached Bilbao in time. There were long queues on the quai.
	__Tues 14th 1995__
		We set off from Portsmouth for Birmingham at 4.40. pm.
pages.notes: Top of page has a packing/shopping list note ('For May half-term need woolly hats, barbeque...') which may be a separate reminder note unrelated to the Spain trip narrative below. The name after 'Thomas +' reads '[?Ster]' — possibly a partial name or abbreviation. 'quai' is spelled as in French (quay). Mon 13th and Tues 14th 1995 match August 1995 calendar. 'Najera' is underlined; this is Nájera in La Rioja, Spain.

pages.full_text:
[Glued-in black-and-white photograph of a Romanesque tympanum, likely from __Vézelay__ basilica]
[?Sennit] 523-6668
[?compositor]
__Vézelay__
pages.notes: Right page is dominated by a glued-in black-and-white photograph of a Romanesque tympanum (semicircular carved stone relief above a doorway), consistent with the famous tympanum of the Basilica of Sainte-Marie-Madeleine at Vézelay, Burgundy, France. Handwritten 'Vézelay' appears in blue ink on the right margin. Bottom of photo/page has printed/stamped text that reads approximately '[?Sennit] 523-6668' and '[?compositor]' — likely postcard printing information, partially legible. The left-margin text visible in this image is gutter overflow from the left page and is not re-transcribed here.
